questions.question: Which term describes any environmental agent that can cause harm to a developing embryo or fetus during prenatal development?
questions.explanation: A teratogen is any environmental agent -- such as alcohol, certain drugs, viruses, or chemicals -- that can cause harm to a developing embryo or fetus. Fetal alcohol spectrum disorders are a well-known example of teratogenic effects.

questions.question: According to research on attachment, a securely attached infant in the Strange Situation typically:

options.text: Clings to the caregiver constantly and refuses to explore the environment
Shows no preference for the caregiver over a stranger
Shows little distress when the caregiver leaves and ignores them upon return
Uses the caregiver as a safe base for exploration and is comforted upon their return

questions.explanation: Securely attached infants use the caregiver as a safe base from which to explore. They may show distress when the caregiver leaves but are quickly comforted upon return. This pattern is associated with consistent, responsive caregiving.
